LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HSI PRODUCTIONS, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM PRAYER FROM FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT SHOW DEFENDANT HAD EITHER ADVANCE KNOWLEDGE OF THE UNFITNESS OF AN EMPLOYEE AND EMPLOYED HIM WITH A CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE RIGHTS OR SAFETY OF OTHERS, OR AUTHORIZED OR RATIFIED THE WRONGFUL CONDUCT FOR WHICH THE DAMAGES ARE AWARDED, OR WAS PERSONALLY GUILTY OF OPPRESSION, FRAUD, OR MALICE. THE COMPLAINT FILED TO ALLEGE ANY OPPRESSION, FRAUD OR MALICE AS REQUIRED BY CIVIL CODE SECTION 3294(B). (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: SCOTT HUGHES, CSR #12365

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HSI PRODUCTIONS, INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS TO SUPPORT A REASONABLE EXPECTATION OF PRIVACY WHILE ON A PUBLIC STREET. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD SPECIFIC FACTS THAT DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEES INTENDED TO CAUSE HARMFUL OR OFFENSIVE CONDUCT OR THAT HE BELIEVED HE WAS ABOUT TO BE TOUCHED IN A HARMFUL OR OFFENSIVE MANNER, OR THAT HE WAS THREATENED TO BE TOUCHED IN A HARMFUL OR OFFENSIVE MANNER. (SEE CACI 1301 AND 1320.)(PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: SCOTT HUGHES, CSR #12365

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-29-2009 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Jul-01-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 1, 2009, line 16, DEFENDANT HSI PRODUCTIONS, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM PRAYER FROM FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to Strike is granted with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege facts that show defendant had either advance knowledge of the unfitness of an employee and employed him with a conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others, or authorized or ratified the wrongful conduct for which the damages are awarded, or was personally guilty of oppression, fraud, or malice. The Complaint filed to allege any oppression, fraud or malice as required by Civil Code section 3294(b). (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Scott Hughes, CSR # 12365

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-01-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT HSI PRODUCTIONS, INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege facts to support a reasonable expectation of privacy while on a public street. Plaintiff must plead specific facts that defendant's employees intended to cause harmful or offensive conduct or that he believed he was about to be touched in a harmful or offensive manner, or that he was threatened to be touched in a harmful or offensive manner. (See CACI 1301 and 1320.)(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Scott Hughes, CSR # 12365

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
